regime_justification: USDJPY near 160 watch line with MOF intervention rhetoric active, BOJ holding April 27-28 with no hike priced, spread intact but vol low, Iran conflict keeping energy elevated—carry fragile but not yet breaking.

domino_stage.label: Pressure Building
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 159.54, 46 pips below 160 MOF line; Katayama warned of decisive action; BOJ meeting Monday with hold expected but June hike 57% priced; JGB 10Y at 2.44% near multi-week high; oil $95.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ meeting Apr 27-28: hold expected but any hawkish June signal or MOF rate-check rumor could spark vol spike; USDJPY 46 pips from 160.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ rhetoric digestion; Iran ceasefire extension fragile; oil elevated; spread 186 bps still supportive but carry-to-vol (24.8) at low end—fragile setup.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: If BOJ signals June hike and USDJPY stays pinned near 160, MOF intervention risk rises; oil volatility remains wildcard; equity resilience key.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If BOJ holds and June pricing fades, spread stabilizes, oil normalizes—carry reset lower but intact; by late May absent catalyst unwind less likely.

bottom_line: Stage 1 (Pressure Building): USDJPY 46 pips below MOF's 160 line, Katayama rhetoric active, BOJ meeting Monday with hold expected but June hike 57% priced. Spread 186 bps, vol 7.5%—carry-to-vol 24.8 at low end. Oil $95, JGB 10Y 2.44%, VIX 19.3, MOVE 67. 1-2w grades C: BOJ catalyst risk and fragile vol setup; 3w C on June hike scenario; 4w D if no trigger materializes. Not yet unwinding, but complacency thin.

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 159.54, 46 pips from 160; Katayama April 24: authorities retain 'free hand', prepared for 'decisive action' vs speculative moves.
Apr 27-28 meeting: 93% expect hold at 0.75%; June hike 57% priced; any hawkish surprise or June signal lifts unwind probability.
JGB 10Y 2.44%, highest since early April, testing Feb '99 2.49% peak; inflation + BOJ tightening path elevating yields.
MOVE 67, well below stress threshold; rates vol calm; would need sharp JGB dislocation or fiscal shock to spike.
Data unavailable today, but vol subdued at 7.5%; if BOJ signals June hike or MOF rate-checks intensify, reversals will skew calls.
¥/bbl crude 15,181 (WTI $95 × 159.54); Hormuz blockade ongoing; Iran talks fragile; oil spike >$110 would breach prior import-cost peaks.

narrative.p1: Quiet Friday tape: USDJPY 159.54 (-0.03%), holding 46 pips below the MOF's 160 intervention watch line. Finance Minister Katayama reiterated April 24 that authorities retain a 'free hand' to intervene and are prepared for 'decisive action' against speculative moves—classic pre-intervention rhetoric. BOJ meets Monday-Tuesday (Apr 27-28); market prices 93% hold at 0.75%, but 57% chance of June hike. JGB 10Y climbed 1 bp to 2.44%, highest in over a week, driven by Friday's core CPI acceleration (1.8% YoY, up from 1.6%) on energy-cost pass-through from Iran conflict. US 10Y 4.30% (-1.9 bps) eased on DOJ dropping Powell probe, but week finished higher on oil/Iran headlines. Spread 186 bps, down 3 from Thursday. Oil gave back some gains Friday ($95.13, -0.76%) on Iran-talks optimism, but still +14% week. Nikkei rallied +1.01% to 59,736 on tech strength, erasing Iran-conflict losses. VIX 19.3 (+2.06%), MOVE 67 (-3.12%)—vol metrics calm. Stage unchanged at 1 (Pressure Building): carry setup fragile but not yet cracking.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 186 bps, JGB 10Y 2.44%, USDJPY 1M vol 7.5%—carry-to-vol = 24.8, at low end of range (carry still pays but vol-adjusted return compressed). USDJPY 5-day change +0.04%, pace slow but level elevated—MOF historically reacts to pace *and* proximity to thresholds; we're 46 pips from 160. Distance from 60d mean: slightly stretched high. ¥/bbl crude 15,181 (WTI $95 × 159.54)—elevated but not panic; Iran ceasefire extended indefinitely but Hormuz blockade persists, so oil tail risk remains. We remain Stage 1 (Pressure Building): BOJ rhetoric shifting (hold Monday but June hike 57% priced), USDJPY near MOF line, JGB yields drifting up, oil elevated. Grades 1-2w C (Watchful): BOJ meeting is binary catalyst—any hawkish June language or MOF rate-check rumors spike vol and tighten spread; USDJPY vol subdued at 7.5% but that's pre-event compression. Grade 3w C: if BOJ signals June and USDJPY stays pinned near 160, intervention risk rises materially. Grade 4w D (Unlikely): if BOJ holds dovish tone and June pricing fades, carry resets lower but stays intact absent exogenous shock. Did NOT apply unwind prior today—data genuinely watchful on its own: proximity to 160 + BOJ meeting + oil overhang = legitimate C grades for 1-3w. Prior would tilt 4w C→C, but current 4w D reflects base case that absent trigger by late May, carry muddles through at lower returns.
narrative.p3: BTC $77,600 (-0.38%), consolidating after +2.8% week; resistance at $80k. ETF inflows resumed (8-day streak, $2B cumulative), institutional bid returning. Open interest data unavailable, but BTC holding above $75k support suggests risk-on rotation intact—crypto correlating with equity resilience (Nikkei +1%, S&P near highs). XRP price unavailable. BTC-DXY correlation unavailable but DXY elevated (Iran premium in dollar); once oil/Iran normalize, weaker dollar could lift BTC. For crypto positioning: $80k break confirms leg higher toward $84-85k; failure and retest of $74k support would signal risk-off resuming. Current setup: BTC tracking equity sentiment, less sensitive to yen carry dynamics than August '24 unwind, but a sharp USDJPY move (intervention or BOJ shock) would cascade into crypto via cross-asset vol and margin calls. Watch Monday BOJ closely—hawkish surprise = risk-off across board; dovish hold = BTC likely tests $80k.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1w C vs 2w C: BOJ meeting Apr 27-28 is immediate binary catalyst (1w), but rhetoric/guidance may not clarify until Summary of Opinions (May 12) or post-meeting Ueda interviews (2w window). If BOJ holds and softens June language, 2w grade could ease to D; if BOJ holds but strengthens June signal, both stay C. USDJPY proximity to 160 means MOF rate-check risk elevated in both windows.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2w C vs 3w C: By week 3 (mid-May), market will have digested BOJ guidance and priced June meeting (Jun 15-16). If June hike stays >50% priced and USDJPY remains near 160, intervention risk compounds—3w stays C. If BOJ dovish and USDJPY drifts to 157-158, 3w eases to D. Oil/Iran resolution timeline also matters: ceasefire collapse in week 3 = oil spike, yen safe-haven bid, paradoxically *helps* carry by weakening dollar less; but energy-driven JGB yield spike tightens spread.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3w C vs 4w D: By week 4 (late May), if no intervention, no BOJ shock, and oil normalizing, carry resets at lower spread (175-180 bps range) but remains positive. 4w D reflects base case that trigger window narrows—June meeting still ahead but not imminent, USDJPY mean-reverts slightly lower, vol fades. Non-monotonic path valid: catalysts cluster in weeks 1-3 (BOJ, MOF, Iran), disperse by week 4 if none fire.

watch_list: BOJ decision & Ueda press conference Apr 28: any mention of 'June' or 'appropriate timing' for next hike flips 1-2w grades to B.
MOF rate-check rumors or Katayama follow-up statement: USDJPY 46 pips from 160; any official FX market inquiry = intervention imminent.
Iran ceasefire breakdown or Hormuz escalation: oil >$110 = ¥/bbl >17,500, inflation spike, JGB sell-off, spread compression catalyst.
USDJPY 1M implied vol: currently 7.5%; spike >10% post-BOJ signals market pricing unwind risk; collapse <6% = complacency extreme.
JGB 10Y >2.50%: would break Feb '99 cycle high, signal fiscal/inflation concerns, force BOJ response—spread compression accelerates.
